binaries.title: msircomm.sys (Microsoft IR/Infrared Communications driver) - Uninitialized PagedPool device-name buffer zeroed before IoGetDeviceProperty (CWE-908), defense-in-depth hardening

changed_functions.name: IrCommHandleSymbolicLink
key_changes: Patched build adds memset(DeviceName.Buffer, 0, DeviceName.MaximumLength + 2) immediately after the second ExAllocatePoolWithTag (0x202 bytes for DeviceName.Buffer) and before IoGetDeviceProperty partially fills it. In the unpatched build this buffer is allocated but NOT zeroed. The memset thunk is 0x1C0006500, called with Val=0.
Register r13 is introduced to hold the constant 2 for the size argument (MaximumLength + 2); the remaining differences are register renumbering from the inserted code.
changed_functions.security_notes: In IrCommHandleSymbolicLink, a 0x202-byte (514) PagedPool buffer for DeviceName.Buffer is allocated with ExAllocatePoolWithTag but, in the unpatched build, is NOT zeroed before IoGetDeviceProperty(DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName) writes only ResultLength bytes (the physical device object name) into it. The tail beyond ResultLength holds stale pool data. This is a CWE-908 (Use of Uninitialized Resource) pattern. However, the buffer's consumers are bounded to the returned name: IoCreateSymbolicLink uses DeviceName as a counted UNICODE_STRING with Length = ResultLength - 2, and RtlWriteRegistryValue / RtlDeleteRegistryValue pass DeviceName.Buffer as a NUL-terminated value name. The uninitialized tail is therefore not surfaced to user mode in the code as written, so no information-disclosure primitive is demonstrable. The patch is a low-severity defense-in-depth zeroing. Reached only via PnP AddDevice / IRP_MN_REMOVE_DEVICE (device attach/detach or driver reinstall), not an unprivileged IOCTL surface.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- msircomm_unpatched.sys/IrCommHandleSymbolicLink (0x1C00016D5..0x1C00016FA)
+++ msircomm_patched.sys/IrCommHandleSymbolicLink (0x1C00016E0..0x1C0001718)
 unpatched: after 'jnz loc_1C00016E1' the code goes straight to IoGetDeviceProperty setup:
   0x1C00016E1 movzx r8d, [rbp+DeviceName.MaximumLength]
   0x1C00016EF mov   r9, rax        ; PropertyBuffer (NOT zeroed)
   0x1C00016FA call  cs:__imp_IoGetDeviceProperty
 patched: a memset is inserted at the jnz target loc_1C00016EC:
+  0x1C00016EC movzx r8d, [rbp+DeviceName.MaximumLength]
+  0x1C00016F1 xor   edx, edx        ; Val = 0
+  0x1C00016F3 add   r8, r13         ; Size = MaximumLength + 2 (r13 = 2)
+  0x1C00016F6 mov   rcx, rax
+  0x1C00016F9 call  memset          ; *** ADDED: zero full 0x202 allocation ***
   0x1C00016FE movzx r8d, [rbp+DeviceName.MaximumLength]
   0x1C0001718 call  cs:__imp_IoGetDeviceProperty

changed_functions.name: DriverEntry
key_changes: Patched build adds memset(PoolWithTag, 0, Length + 2) between ExAllocatePoolWithTag and the memmove that copies RegistryPath. The buffer is already fully written in both builds (memmove of Length bytes + a NUL-terminator word store at offset Length covers the whole Length+2 allocation), so this memset is redundant.
The prologue gains an extra saved register (r15) and the allocation size (Length+2) is cached in r14 for the new memset call.
changed_functions.security_notes: In DriverEntry, a PagedPool buffer of (RegistryPath.Length + 2) bytes is allocated and, in BOTH builds, fully initialized before use: memmove(sub_1c00061c0) copies RegistryPath.Length bytes, then '*(WORD*)(buf + Length/2) = 0' writes a NUL terminator into the final 2 bytes, covering the entire allocation. The patched build adds memset(buf, 0, Length+2) before the memmove, but since every byte is subsequently written there is no uninitialized/residue window in either build. This is a redundant defense-in-depth change with no security effect; CWE-908 does not apply because no uninitialized byte is ever used.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- msircomm_unpatched.sys/DriverEntry (0x1C000C07C..0x1C000C0A0)
+++ msircomm_patched.sys/DriverEntry (0x1C000C07E..0x1C000C0AF)
 unpatched:
   0x1C000C07C jz    loc_1C000C254
   0x1C000C082 movzx r8d, word ptr [rsi]   ; Size = RegistryPath.Length
   0x1C000C089 call  memmove
   0x1C000C0A0 mov   [rdi+rcx*2], r14w      ; NUL word at offset Length (fills last 2 bytes)
 patched:
   0x1C000C07E jz    loc_1C000C263
+  0x1C000C084 mov   r8d, r14d              ; Size = Length + 2
+  0x1C000C087 xor   edx, edx               ; Val = 0
+  0x1C000C089 mov   rcx, rax
+  0x1C000C08C call  memset                 ; *** ADDED (redundant) ***
   0x1C000C091 movzx r8d, word ptr [rsi]    ; Size = RegistryPath.Length
   0x1C000C09C call  memmove
   0x1C000C0AF mov   [rdi+rcx*2], r15w      ; NUL word at offset Length

security_findings.entry_point: PnP AddDevice (IrCommAddDevice 0x1C0001010) and IRP_MJ_PNP IRP_MN_REMOVE_DEVICE (IrCommPnP 0x1C0001290, MinorFunction==2)
call_chain: PnP Manager AddDevice -> IrCommAddDevice (0x1C0001010) -> IrCommHandleSymbolicLink(PhysicalDeviceObject, InterfaceName, create)
security_findings.description: IrCommHandleSymbolicLink allocates a 0x202-byte (514) PagedPool buffer via ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, 0x202, 'IrCo') for DeviceName.Buffer. In the unpatched build the buffer is not zeroed before IoGetDeviceProperty(DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName) writes only ResultLength bytes (the physical device object name) into it, leaving the tail with stale pool data. The patched build adds memset(DeviceName.Buffer, 0, DeviceName.MaximumLength + 2) before IoGetDeviceProperty. This is a genuine CWE-908 hardening, but the downstream consumers are bounded to the returned name and do not expose the uninitialized tail: IoCreateSymbolicLink(&Destination, &DeviceName) uses DeviceName as a counted UNICODE_STRING with Length = ResultLength - 2 (exactly the PDO name); RtlWriteRegistryValue(4, "SERIALCOMM", DeviceName.Buffer, ...) and RtlDeleteRegistryValue(4, "SERIALCOMM", DeviceName.Buffer) treat DeviceName.Buffer as a NUL-terminated value name and stop at the terminator IoGetDeviceProperty placed within ResultLength. No user-mode-observable disclosure of the uninitialized bytes is demonstrable.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. msircomm.sys loaded (IR hardware present or force-loaded). 2. PnP AddDevice runs the create path (a3 != 0); IRP_MN_REMOVE_DEVICE runs the delete path (a3 == 0). Both are driven by device attach/detach or driver reinstall, not by an unprivileged IOCTL. 3. In the unpatched build the bytes of DeviceName.Buffer beyond ResultLength hold stale pool data at the IoGetDeviceProperty return, but the symbolic-link and registry consumers operate on the bounded PDO name only.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. Defense-in-depth zeroing of a partially-filled PagedPool buffer; the uninitialized tail does not reach a user-readable artifact in the code as written. No pool-grooming, KASLR-bypass, or mitigation-bypass primitive is supported by the observed paths.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED IrCommHandleSymbolicLink second allocation and use
00000001C00016B0  mov     edx, 202h                          ; NumberOfBytes = 514
00000001C00016C2  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
00000001C00016CE  mov     [rbp+DeviceName.Buffer], rax
00000001C00016D2  test    rax, rax
00000001C00016D5  jnz     short loc_1C00016E1                ; no memset in unpatched
00000001C00016E1  movzx   r8d, [rbp+DeviceName.MaximumLength]; BufferLength
00000001C00016EF  mov     r9, rax                            ; PropertyBuffer (NOT zeroed)
00000001C00016F5  mov     edx, 0Bh                           ; DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName
00000001C00016FA  call    cs:__imp_IoGetDeviceProperty
00000001C0001710  movzx   eax, word ptr [rbp+arg_18]         ; ResultLength
00000001C0001714  sub     ax, 2
00000001C0001718  add     [rbp+DeviceName.Length], ax        ; Length = ResultLength - 2
00000001C000171C  test    r14b, r14b                         ; create vs delete
00000001C0001725  lea     rdx, [rbp+DeviceName]              ; counted UNICODE_STRING
00000001C000172D  call    cs:__imp_IoCreateSymbolicLink      ; target bounded by Length
00000001C000174F  mov     r8, [rbp+DeviceName.Buffer]        ; ValueName (NUL-terminated)
00000001C0001768  call    cs:__imp_RtlWriteRegistryValue
; PATCHED inserts at loc_1C00016EC (memset at 0x1C00016F9) before IoGetDeviceProperty (0x1C0001718)

breakpoints.why: Entry to the device symbolic-link routine. rcx = DeviceObject, rdx = InterfaceName pointer, r8b = 1 (create) / 0 (delete).

Second 0x202 allocation for DeviceName.Buffer (rax). In the unpatched build no zero-fill follows before IoGetDeviceProperty at 0x1C00016FA; in the patched build a memset (0x1C00016F9) precedes IoGetDeviceProperty (0x1C0001718).
AddDevice caller of the routine (create path).
PnP dispatch; at MinorFunction==2 (IRP_MN_REMOVE_DEVICE) it calls the routine on the delete path.

watch: rax after 0x1C00016C2 - DeviceName.Buffer pointer (unpatched: uninitialized tail beyond ResultLength)
key_offsets: 0x1C00016D5 - jnz loc_1C00016E1: unpatched goes straight to IoGetDeviceProperty setup
0x1C00016F9 - memset added in patched build (before IoGetDeviceProperty at 0x1C0001718)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Load msircomm.sys and drive a PnP AddDevice (create path) or IRP_MN_REMOVE_DEVICE (delete path) via device attach/detach or reinstall. The create path calls IrCommAddDevice (0x1C0001010) -> IrCommHandleSymbolicLink; the delete path calls IrCommPnP (0x1C0001290) -> IrCommHandleSymbolicLink.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: After IoGetDeviceProperty in the unpatched build, bytes of DeviceName.Buffer beyond ResultLength are non-zero pool residue; in the patched build they are zero. The symbolic link target and the SERIALCOMM registry value name contain only the physical device object name in both builds, because those consumers are bounded by the counted length / NUL terminator.
debugging_aids.notes: The first buffer (Destination.Buffer, 0x1C00016... first allocation) is zeroed in BOTH builds via the memset at 0x1C0001637 (unpatched) / 0x1C0001642 (patched). The change is limited to the second allocation (DeviceName.Buffer). Pool tag 'IrCo' (0x6F437249, little-endian). The driver PE has stack cookies and _guard_dispatch_icall present.

security_findings.description: In DriverEntry a PagedPool buffer of (RegistryPath->Length + 2) bytes is allocated to copy the registry path. In BOTH builds it is fully initialized before use: memmove copies RegistryPath->Length bytes and the store '*(WORD*)(buf + Length/2) = 0' writes a NUL terminator into the final 2 bytes, covering the whole allocation. The patched build adds memset(buf, 0, Length+2) before the memmove, which is redundant because every byte is subsequently written. There is no uninitialized/residue window in either build, so this is not an information-disclosure fix.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: DriverEntry runs once at driver load. The registry-path buffer is fully written in both builds, so there is nothing to trigger.
security_findings.primitive: None. Redundant hardening with no residue window.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED DriverEntry
00000001C000C06A  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
00000001C000C076  mov     rdi, rax
00000001C000C07C  jz      loc_1C000C254
00000001C000C082  movzx   r8d, word ptr [rsi]                ; Size = RegistryPath.Length
00000001C000C089  call    memmove
00000001C000C0A0  mov     [rdi+rcx*2], r14w                  ; NUL word at offset Length (fills last 2 bytes)
; PATCHED inserts before the memmove:
; 0x1C000C084 mov r8d, r14d ; Size = Length+2
; 0x1C000C087 xor edx, edx
; 0x1C000C089 mov rcx, rax
; 0x1C000C08C call memset   ; redundant

breakpoints.why: Driver load. rcx = DriverObject, rdx = RegistryPath (UNICODE_STRING*: +0 Length, +8 Buffer).

watch: rdi after 0x1C000C076 - the registry-path pool buffer
key_offsets: 0x1C000C0A0 - NUL-terminator store covering the final 2 bytes (both builds)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Load the driver (sc start / pnputil install). One-time initialization event.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In both builds the buffer is fully written by the memmove plus NUL-terminator store; the patched memset merely zeroes it first.
debugging_aids.notes: Redundant hardening. No uninitialized byte is ever used in either build.

summary: An independent function-by-function diff of both builds confirms exactly two content changes, both memset insertions after ExAllocatePoolWithTag. In IrCommHandleSymbolicLink (0x1C00015C0) the patch zeroes a 0x202-byte PagedPool device-name buffer before IoGetDeviceProperty partially fills it - a genuine but low-impact CWE-908 hardening, because the buffer's consumers (a counted-length IoCreateSymbolicLink and a NUL-terminated RtlWriteRegistryValue/RtlDeleteRegistryValue value name) are bounded to the returned physical device object name and never surface the uninitialized tail to user mode. In DriverEntry (0x1C000C008) the added memset zeroes a registry-path buffer that is already fully initialized in both builds (memmove plus NUL-terminator store), so it is a redundant defense-in-depth change with no security effect. No demonstrable information-disclosure primitive, and no KASLR-bypass or pool-grooming chain, is supported by the observed code.
